extractions.problem: Addressing the statistical and computational challenges inherent in building whole cell models.
How to introduce and teach systems biology methods for understanding complex cellular systems involving hundreds or thousands of gene products.
extractions.method: Textbook covering modeling methods including synthetic circuits, transcriptional, signaling, metabolic, and integrated multi-network models.
extractions.finding: Provides a pedagogical framework progressing from synthetic biological circuits to whole-cell models, building intuition about strengths and weaknesses of each modeling method.

extractions.problem: Developing a mathematical framework for modeling biological cells from a physicochemical perspective, including self-replication.
extractions.method: A whole-cell modeling framework with cytosolic volume (well-stirred reactor) and membrane surface, where volume and surface area are coupled to reaction dynamics.
extractions.finding: Two self-replicating protocell models were constructed; growth and division arise naturally from chemical mechanisms without external enforcement, achieving stable oscillations synchronized to volume and surface area doubling.
extractions.dataset: Prototypical whole-cell models (minimal and eukaryotic cell cycle)
extractions.metric: Stable oscillations in concentrations and surface-to-volume ratio
extractions.result: Self-replicative behavior achieved via synchronized oscillations

extractions.problem: Comparing reductive reactivities of microbial and mammalian cytochromes P-450 with heme and whole-cell models for biodehalogenation.
extractions.method: Comparative biochemical assays of cytochromes P-450 reductive dehalogenation activity across microbial, mammalian, heme, and whole-cell systems.
extractions.dataset: Microbial and mammalian cytochromes P-450, heme models

extractions.problem: Current meniscal lesion treatments fail to prevent adverse effects; tissue engineering may offer an alternative for whole meniscus regeneration.
extractions.method: Biodegradable PGA scaffolds reinforced with PLGA, seeded with allogeneic meniscal cells, cultured in vitro and transplanted into rabbit knee joints after total meniscectomy.
extractions.finding: Neomenisci formed with fibrocartilage regeneration at 6-10 weeks, with type I and II collagen similar to normal tissue, though differences in collagen content and aggregate modulus remained at 36 weeks.
extractions.dataset: Rabbit total meniscectomy model
extractions.metric: Compressive modulus, collagen content, aggregate modulus, immunohistochemistry
extractions.result: 28-fold higher compressive modulus for bonded vs nonbonded scaffold; neomenisci collagen and modulus differ from native

extractions.problem: Understanding how inherent randomness in biomolecular movements and reactivity gives rise to large-scale behavioral differences in otherwise identical cells.
extractions.method: Lattice Microbes (GPU-accelerated stochastic reaction-diffusion simulator) for microscopic kinetics, and population flux balance analysis (FBA) for systems-level metabolic phenotype variability.
extractions.finding: Population FBA predicted variability in E. coli metabolic pathway usage from protein expression heterogeneity; hybrid FBA-kinetic simulations enable study of cell competition and cooperation in colonies.
extractions.dataset: Escherichia coli

extractions.problem: Little is known about the bioavailability of carotenoids from whole foods despite epidemiological evidence linking carotenoid-rich diets to reduced chronic disease risk.
extractions.method: In vitro digestion coupled with Caco-2 human intestinal cell culture model to assess carotenoid absorption from carrots and corn.
extractions.finding: Caco-2 uptake of beta-carotene and zeaxanthin was higher than lutein; uptake was curvilinear and saturated after 4 hours; cooked corn significantly enhanced carotenoid bioavailability.
extractions.dataset: Carrots, whole grain corn, Caco-2 cells
extractions.metric: Cellular uptake kinetics, dose response
extractions.result: Saturation at 4h incubation; cooked corn enhanced bioavailability

extractions.problem: Interpreting complex multi-compartment metabolomic and genotype data for individual patients remains an unresolved challenge for personalized medicine.
extractions.method: Multi-omic, data-driven, personalized whole-cell kinetic models of erythrocyte metabolism constructed from fasting-state plasma/erythrocyte metabolomics and whole-genome genotyping for 24 healthy individuals.
extractions.finding: Personalized kinetic rate constants better represent genotype than metabolite levels; models identified individuals at risk for ribavirin-induced anemia and showed how ITPA deficiency may protect against this side effect.
extractions.dataset: 24 healthy individuals, plasma/erythrocyte metabolomics, whole-genome genotyping
extractions.metric: Genotype-phenotype correlation, drug side effect risk prediction
extractions.result: Kinetic rate constants outperform metabolite levels for genotype representation

extractions.problem: Reconstructing whole-cell models for systems-level understanding of cellular function.
The t(11;14) translocation alone is insufficient for mantle cell lymphoma (MCL); secondary genomic alterations need to be comprehensively identified.
extractions.method: Whole genome array CGH using a DNA microarray of 32,433 overlapping genomic segments spanning the entire human genome, applied to 8 MCL cell lines.
extractions.finding: An average of 35 genetic alterations per cell line were identified, with recurrent amplifications at 18q21 (BCL2) and 13q31 (GPC5), homozygous deletion at 9p21 (p15/p16), and 13 novel minimally altered regions.
extractions.dataset: 8 MCL cell lines (Granta-519, HBL-2, NCEB-1, Rec-1, SP49, UPN-1, Z138C, JVM-2)
extractions.metric: Tiling resolution, recurrent gain/loss detection
extractions.result: Average 35 alterations/cell line, 14 recurrent losses, 21 recurrent gains, 13 novel regions, detection down to 130 kb

extractions.problem: Building 3D structural models of entire cells requires unprecedented integration of multiple biological data sources and enhanced computational methods.
extractions.method: CellPACK suite for computational modeling, combining experimental and homology-modeled structures with lattice-based genome models to build 3D models of a whole Mycoplasma genitalium cell.
extractions.finding: First 3D structural models of an entire M. genitalium cell were built; both manual and automated workflows are appropriate for mesoscale analysis of crowding and volume occupancy.
extractions.dataset: Mycoplasma genitalium proteome, DNA, RNA
extractions.metric: Model quality, volume occupancy, crowding analysis
extractions.result: First complete 3D structural model of M. genitalium

extractions.problem: Population density approaches to modeling local control of Ca2+-induced Ca2+ release scale poorly with the number of ryanodine receptors per Ca2+ release unit.
extractions.method: Langevin description of collective RyR gating coupled by local Ca2+ concentration, combined with Fokker-Planck equations for a local/global whole cell model of permeabilized ventricular myocytes.
extractions.finding: Elevated myoplasmic Ca2+ promotes SR Ca2+ uptake but also activates RyRs; increasing myoplasmic Ca2+ causes exponential increase in spark-mediated release and linear increase in nonspark-mediated release, consistent with experiments.
extractions.dataset: Permeabilized ventricular myocytes (model)
extractions.metric: Spark-mediated vs nonspark-mediated release flux
extractions.result: Exponential increase in spark-mediated release, linear increase in nonspark-mediated release; two steady-state release fluxes

extractions.problem: Investigating the direct role of TNF-alpha in obesity-linked insulin resistance.
extractions.method: TNF-alpha mRNA expression analysis in adipose tissue from four rodent models of obesity/diabetes, plus TNF-alpha neutralization experiments in obese fa/fa rats.
extractions.finding: TNF-alpha mRNA and protein were elevated in adipose tissue of obese rodent models; neutralization of TNF-alpha significantly increased peripheral glucose uptake in response to insulin.
extractions.dataset: Four rodent models of obesity and diabetes, obese fa/fa rats
extractions.metric: Peripheral glucose uptake in response to insulin
extractions.baseline: Obese fa/fa rats without TNF-alpha neutralization
extractions.result: Significant increase in insulin-stimulated glucose uptake after TNF-alpha neutralization

extractions.problem: Tracking the evolving shape of whole fluorescent cells in time-lapse microscopy is challenging due to noise and complex morphologies.
extractions.method: Coherence-enhancing diffusion filtering combined with Chan-Vese model minimization in fast level set-like and graph cut frameworks, integrated with a topological prior for multiple cell tracking.
extractions.finding: The tracking scheme successfully segments and tracks multiple cells in 2D and 3D time-lapse series of rat adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells and human lung squamous cell carcinoma cells.
extractions.dataset: Rat adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells (2D), human lung squamous cell carcinoma cells (3D)

extractions.problem: Understanding the uptake, trafficking, and toxic effects of plastic nanoparticles at cellular and systemic levels in aquatic species.
extractions.method: Zebrafish (Danio rerio) cell line and larvae exposure to polystyrene nanoparticles of different sizes (50 nm and 1 μm), with analysis of endocytic mechanisms, ROS, gene expression, and immune cell migration.
extractions.finding: 50 nm NPs distributed homogeneously in cytoplasm via dynamin-dependent internalization; 1 μm NPs agglomerated via phagocytosis; both degraded in lysosomes causing alkalinization; NPs accumulated in gut, triggered ROS, and decreased survival in immunodeficient/infected larvae.
extractions.dataset: Zebrafish cell line (ZFL), zebrafish larvae (wild-type and immunodeficient)
extractions.metric: ROS levels, catalase expression, immune cell migration, survival rate
extractions.result: Decreased survival in immunodeficient/infected larvae upon NP exposure; lysosomal alkalinization

extractions.problem: Integrating computational, algorithmic, and implementational levels of analysis into a single platform for realistic simulation of neural information processing is difficult.
extractions.method: Multiscale dynamical 'whole-cell' models for fly photoreceptors integrating molecular-level processes with systems-level function, following Marr's three levels of analysis.
extractions.finding: Models uncovered mechanisms and evolutionary rules of quantal light information sampling and integration underlying light adaptation, improving understanding of insect vision.
extractions.dataset: Fly (Drosophila) photoreceptors

extractions.problem: Current acellular pertussis vaccines do not prevent colonization or transmission of B. pertussis; understanding if whole-cell vaccine benefits are generalizable across manufacturers.
extractions.method: Baboon model of pertussis comparing three whole-cell vaccines from different manufacturers against acellular vaccine and unvaccinated controls, measuring nasopharyngeal clearance and IL-17 transcription.
extractions.finding: All three whole-cell vaccines significantly accelerated B. pertussis clearance and reduced nasopharyngeal burden; whole-cell vaccines induced IL-17 (Th17) responses unlike acellular vaccines; previously infected animals were not colonized upon reinfection.
extractions.dataset: Baboon model, B. pertussis challenge
extractions.metric: Nasopharyngeal clearance rate, B. pertussis burden, IL-17 transcription
extractions.result: Significant acceleration of clearance and reduction of burden with all 3 whole-cell vaccines

extractions.problem: The default constant-volume, steady-state reactor framework is unsatisfactory for modeling growth and division of cells.
extractions.method: A whole-cell modeling framework with expanding volumes (cytoplasm and membrane), osmotic pressure-driven growth, and a cell-division cycle with equatorial constriction.
extractions.finding: The framework itself imposes periodic/oscillatory behavior on all components due to differential volume change rates, yielding more realistic concentration dynamics than constant-volume models.
extractions.dataset: Symbolic whole cell model (spherical cell)
extractions.metric: Periodicity, stability of oscillatory solutions
extractions.result: Stable periodic solutions without requiring inherent biochemical oscillations

extractions.problem: Developing a whole-cell biosensor for screening toxins, using histamine as a model.
extractions.method: Potentiometric biosensor with HUVEC monolayer on RGD-modified cellulose triacetate membrane over an ion-selective electrode; toxin-induced permeability changes allow ion diffusion and measurable potential response.
extractions.finding: Measured potential increased with histamine concentration while L-histidine did not affect permeability, demonstrating selectivity; applicable to medical, food, environmental, and security fields.
extractions.dataset: HUVEC monolayer, histamine and L-histidine
extractions.metric: Potentiometric response (mV), selectivity vs L-histidine
extractions.result: Dose-dependent response to histamine; no response to L-histidine

extractions.problem: Understanding the intracellular distribution and translocation dynamics of the glucocorticoid receptor in GH1 cells.
extractions.method: Dense amino acid labeling, sucrose gradient sedimentation, and whole cell studies to characterize 10S/4S/3.5S receptor forms and their equilibrium dynamics.
extractions.finding: The 10S receptor is an oligomer in rapidly exchanging equilibrium with its 3.5S subunit; hormone binding shifts equilibrium toward the 4S form which binds DNA and translocates to the nucleus. A new equilibrium model for receptor translocation is proposed.
extractions.dataset: GH1 rat pituitary cells, triamcinolone acetonide
extractions.metric: Sedimentation coefficient (S), receptor half-life
extractions.result: Receptor half-life 9.5h; equilibrium between 10S oligomer and 3.5S/4S subunit

extractions.problem: Modeling complex genetic regulatory circuits within a variable-volume whole-cell framework is difficult due to hierarchical cellular organization.
extractions.method: Lumped dynamic modular representation of a designed bistable switch (two gene expression modules) using Hill-type kinetics in a variable-volume whole-cell framework mimicking E. coli growth.
extractions.finding: The approach allows analysis of stability strength, species connectivity, responsiveness, and regulatory efficiency under stationary and dynamic perturbations, though limitations exist.
extractions.dataset: E. coli (in silico model)
extractions.metric: Stability strength, responsiveness, regulatory efficiency

extractions.problem: Understanding the experimentally observed lateral redistribution of platelets in concentrated red blood cell suspensions during channel flow.
extractions.method: Immersed Boundary method with lattice Boltzmann fluid calculation to simulate red-blood-cell-induced lateral platelet motion in channel flow.
extractions.finding: Simulations capture RBC-induced lateral platelet motion and near-wall enhanced platelet concentration developing within ~400 ms from initial random/uniform distributions.
extractions.dataset: Computational model of whole blood in channel flow
extractions.metric: Platelet concentration profile, near-wall concentration enhancement
extractions.baseline: Random RBC distribution, uniform platelet distribution (initial condition)
extractions.result: Near-wall enhanced platelet concentration develops within ~400 ms

extractions.problem: Designing a whole-cell bioluminescence biosensor for water toxicity detection with sensitive optical detection.
extractions.method: Genetically engineered E. coli (recA::luxCDABE) with single photon avalanche diode (SPAD) in Geiger mode; mathematical model for SOS bioluminescence kinetics.
extractions.finding: Bioluminescence signal initially depends on time squared and spectral intensity is inversely proportional to frequency; excellent agreement between model and measured signal; matched digital filter improved detection.
extractions.dataset: E. coli recA::luxCDABE, toxicant concentrations (5, 15, 20 ppm)
extractions.metric: Bioluminescence signal intensity, time-dependence
extractions.result: Successful detection at 5, 15, and 20 ppm; signal ∝ t²

extractions.problem: Developing in vitro 3D culture systems that generate complex organ-like structures (organoids) from stem cells for biomedical research.
extractions.method: 3D culture systems enabling stem cell-derived or tissue progenitor cells to self-organize into organoids, building on classical reaggregation experiments.
extractions.finding: Organoids create significant prospects for modeling development and diseases, toxicology, drug discovery, and regenerative medicine, though current limitations remain.
extractions.dataset: Human stem cells, patient-derived iPSCs

extractions.problem: Assessing the cytotoxicity and genotoxicity of Moringa oleifera seeds used as a coagulation reagent for drinking water purification.
extractions.method: Acinetobacter whole-cell bioreporter for cytotoxicity and genotoxicity assessment; direct contact, ethanolic-water extraction, and hexane extraction to distinguish hydrophobic/hydrophilic toxic components.
extractions.finding: Hydrophobic lipids contributed dominant cytotoxicity; genotoxicity was dominant in the water-soluble fraction; toxicity showed remarkable synergistic effects.
extractions.dataset: Moringa oleifera seeds (1-1000 mg/L), Acinetobacter bioreporter
extractions.metric: LC50, LC90, genotoxicity equivalence
extractions.result: LC50=8.5 mg/L, LC90=300 mg/L, genotoxicity equivalent to 8.3 mg mitomycin C per 1.0 g dry seed

extractions.problem: Establishing an in vitro model for studying programmed cell death during mammary gland involution.
extractions.method: Comparison of DNA fragmentation and gene expression in involuting mouse mammary glands in vivo vs whole organ culture in vitro with lactogenic hormone withdrawal.
extractions.finding: Non-random DNA degradation and similar gene expression patterns (beta-casein, TGF-beta 1/2/3) were observed both in vivo and in vitro, validating whole organ culture as a useful model for studying involution.
extractions.dataset: Mouse mammary glands (in vivo and whole organ culture)
extractions.metric: DNA fragmentation patterns, gene expression (beta-casein, TGF-beta 1/2/3)
extractions.result: Similar DNA degradation and gene expression patterns in vivo and in vitro

extractions.problem: Determining the impact of switching from whole-cell to acellular pertussis vaccines on the incidence of B. parapertussis infections.
extractions.method: Mouse respiratory model comparing two commercial acellular and two whole-cell vaccines for efficacy against B. parapertussis.
extractions.finding: Whole-cell vaccines protected significantly better than acellular vaccines against B. parapertussis; acellular vaccines showed highly significant enhancement of B. parapertussis colonization later in infection.
extractions.dataset: Mouse respiratory model, B. parapertussis, B. pertussis
extractions.metric: Colonization levels, protection efficacy
extractions.result: Whole-cell vaccines significantly better than acellular; acellular vaccines enhanced B. parapertussis colonization

extractions.problem: Assessing the digestive stability and intestinal absorption of lycopene and alpha-tocopherol from a whole food.
extractions.method: Dynamic TNO gastrointestinal tract model (TIM) coupled with Caco-2 cell monolayers for the first time to evaluate bioaccessibility and absorption from red tomato and sunflower oil.
extractions.finding: Alpha-tocopherol was stable during digestion but lycopene showed 25% loss; absorption of both was curvilinear, bidirectional, and concentration-dependent; lycopene showed lower bioavailability than alpha-tocopherol.
extractions.dataset: Red tomatoes and sunflower oil, Caco-2 cells, TIM system
extractions.metric: Digestive stability, absorption percentage, dose-response
extractions.baseline: Pure compounds vs whole food digesta
extractions.result: 25% lycopene loss during digestion; lower bioavailability for lycopene vs alpha-tocopherol

extractions.problem: Understanding the roles of heat-shock proteins (Hsps) and molecular chaperones in stress responses across ecological and evolutionary contexts.
extractions.method: Review of Hsp expression studies in model and nonmodel organisms under natural and experimental stress conditions.
extractions.finding: Hsp expression occurs in nature, varies across species, correlates with stress resistance, and species' thresholds for Hsp expression correlate with natural stress levels.
extractions.dataset: Multiple model and nonmodel eukaryotic organisms
extractions.result: Hsp expression thresholds correlate with natural stress levels

extractions.problem: Developing a whole-cell model of ribosome biogenesis focusing on kinetic modeling of small subunit (SSU) assembly.
Bridging the gap between 2D cell cultures and whole-animal systems for studying tissue morphogenesis, stem cells, and cancer.
extractions.method: Three-dimensional in vitro models mimicking features of the in vivo environment, combined with traditional cell culture tools.
extractions.finding: 3D models provide unique perspectives on stem cell behavior, developing tissues/organs, and tumors, and may accelerate translational research in cancer biology and tissue engineering.

extractions.problem: Developing a contactless dielectrophoresis (cDEP) platform capable of continuously sorting cancer cells from dilute whole blood samples.
extractions.method: cDEP device with fluid electrodes isolated by thin membrane; analytical model as network of parallel RC pairs; finite element methods for geometry evaluation; operation below 100 kHz.
extractions.finding: First cDEP device operating below 100 kHz successfully manipulated human leukemia cells while red blood cells were unaffected, enabling continuous sorting from dilute blood.
extractions.dataset: Human leukemia cells, dilute whole blood
extractions.metric: Cell manipulation at specified frequencies
extractions.result: First successful cDEP below 100 kHz; leukemia cells sorted, RBCs unaffected

extractions.problem: Quantitatively predicting in vivo hepatic transport and elimination from in vitro uptake data for BDDCS class 3 drugs.
extractions.method: Mechanistic modeling of in vitro hepatic uptake transport in plated rat hepatocytes, scaled to a fully mechanistic PBPK model for in vivo elimination prediction.
extractions.finding: Scaled in vitro uptake clearance required a 10-fold empirical scaling factor; plasma clearance, half-life, max liver concentration, and bile excretion fraction were predicted within 2-fold.
extractions.dataset: Fexofenadine and napsagatran in rat hepatocytes and in vivo rat
extractions.metric: Plasma clearance, half-life, Cmax liver, fraction excreted in bile
extractions.result: CL_P, t_1/2, C_maxL, f_bile predicted within 2-fold; 10x empirical scaling factor needed

extractions.problem: Whole-cell bioluminescent biosensors suffer from inherent problems in overall system light collection efficiency.
extractions.method: Optical ray tracing simulation of bioluminescence emission, transfer, and detection in microbial whole cell biochips, optimizing system geometry and bacterial concentration.
extractions.finding: Optimal detector radius between 300-750 μm and bacterial fixation radius between 800-1200 μm were identified for maximizing light collection.
extractions.dataset: Microbial whole cell biochip (simulated)
extractions.metric: Light collection efficiency
extractions.result: Optimal detector radius 300-750 μm, bacterial fixation radius 800-1200 μm

extractions.problem: How single mitochondrial flickering events and whole-cell mitochondrial oscillations are mechanistically linked remains unclear.
extractions.method: Markov model of inner membrane anion channel with ROS-induced opening, coupled into a mitochondrial network model with ROS-induced ROS release.
extractions.finding: As superoxide production increases, random flickering transitions through short-lived waves to recurring propagating waves via self-organized criticality (power-law cluster distribution), ultimately producing whole-cell oscillations.
extractions.dataset: Mitochondrial network model (in silico)
extractions.metric: Power-law distribution of depolarization clusters, periodicity of oscillations
extractions.result: Power-law distribution at critical state; transition from random flickering to periodic oscillations

extractions.problem: Examining the ability of S. aureus peptidoglycan and lipoteichoic acid to induce cytokine release and identifying the cellular origins of these cytokines.
extractions.method: Human whole blood model stimulated with PepG and LTA; immunomagnetic separation of cell populations; RT-PCR for cytokine mRNA; CD14 receptor blockade.
extractions.finding: Both T cells and monocytes produce IL-6 and IL-10 in response to PepG; PepG is a potent TNF-alpha inducer; CD14 blockade attenuated LTA-induced but not PepG-induced TNF-alpha release.
extractions.dataset: Human whole blood, S. aureus PepG and LTA
extractions.metric: Cytokine levels (TNF-alpha, IL-6, IL-10), mRNA transcripts
extractions.result: TNF-alpha peak at 6h, IL-10 peak at 12h; dose-dependent response

extractions.problem: Isolating and characterizing murine hematopoietic stem cells (HSC) that are replicating in vivo.
extractions.method: Hoechst 33342 vital dye staining with dual-emission-wavelength display to identify side population (SP) cells; competitive repopulation experiments; verapamil blockade of MDR-mediated efflux.
extractions.finding: SP cells contain the vast majority of HSC activity, are enriched ≥1,000-fold for reconstitution activity, contribute to lymphoid and myeloid lineages, and 1-3% are in S-G2M phase with reconstitution capacity equivalent to quiescent stem cells.
extractions.dataset: Murine bone marrow cells
extractions.metric: Fold enrichment for reconstitution activity, DNA content (cell cycle)
extractions.result: ≥1,000-fold enrichment; 1-3% HSC in S-G2M

extractions.problem: Developing a mathematical model of a whole-cell-based bio-chip as an electrochemical biosensor for water toxicity detection.
Deterministic Hodgkin-Huxley models fail to reproduce the irregular intrinsic behavior observed in bursting neurons of the crustacean stomatogastric ganglion.
extractions.method: Stochastic Hodgkin-Huxley-type whole cell model based on microscopic Markovian ion channel states, analyzed with nonlinear dynamics tools.
extractions.finding: The stochastic model reproduces the irregularities, sensitivity to initial conditions, and low-dimensional dynamics found in isolated STG neurons, arising naturally from ion channel stochasticity and nonlinear interactions.
extractions.dataset: Crustacean stomatogastric ganglion (STG) neuron model
extractions.metric: Sensitivity to initial conditions, dimensionality of dynamics
extractions.result: Stochastic model reproduces experimental irregularities without unrealistic dynamics

extractions.problem: Modeling the entire expanded-bed adsorption process for protein purification directly from yeast cell-containing feedstock.
extractions.method: Mathematical model with independently determined parameters for expanded bed adsorption of BSA from yeast cell suspensions using DEAE Spherodex M anion-exchanger; steric mass-action isotherm for elution.
extractions.finding: Dynamic binding capacity of BSA reached 86% of equilibrium despite 6% yeast cells due to small adsorbent size and high pore diffusivity; model predictions agreed well with experimental results for the whole process.
extractions.dataset: BSA with 6% yeast cell suspension, DEAE Spherodex M
extractions.metric: Dynamic binding capacity, axial dispersion coefficient
extractions.result: 86% dynamic binding capacity; axial dispersion ~10⁻⁶ m²/s; good model-experiment agreement

extractions.problem: Determining the influence of aging and heat treatment on the ability of whole yeast cells and yeast cell walls to remove ochratoxin A from wine.
extractions.method: Wine model system with 10 μg/L OTA, 1 g/L whole yeast cells or yeast cell walls, monitored over 214 hours with and without heat pretreatment (85°C, 10 min).
extractions.finding: YCW mannoproteins were involved in OTA removal via adsorption; aging affected WY but not YCW removal capacity; heat treatment increased removal capacity for both.
extractions.dataset: Wine model system, OTA (10 μg/L), whole yeast cells and yeast cell walls
extractions.metric: OTA removal capacity
extractions.result: Heat treatment (85°C, 10 min) increased OTA removal capacity

extractions.problem: Investigating allogeneic whole-tumour cell vaccination as cancer immunotherapy in a rat prostate cancer model.
extractions.method: Crossover vaccination of irradiated MAT-LyLu and PAIII tumour cells between Copenhagen and Lobund-Wistar rats, with SRL172 (heat-killed M. vaccae) as adjuvant.
extractions.finding: Prophylactic allogeneic vaccination provided 80% survival >5 months (vs 40% autologous) with prolonged immunity; SRL172 adjuvant was needed against the more aggressive tumour; therapeutic vaccination showed limited regression.
extractions.dataset: Rat prostate cancer (MAT-LyLu, PAIII cell lines), Copenhagen and Lobund-Wistar rats
extractions.metric: Survival rate, tumour regression, rechallenge protection
extractions.result: 80% survival >5 months with allogeneic vaccine; protected at 5-month rechallenge

extractions.problem: Quantifying surface complexation of Np(V) onto whole cells and cell components of Shewanella alga for understanding actinide-microbiological interactions.
extractions.method: Acid-base titrations with FITEQL modeling for surface functional groups; batch Np(V) sorption experiments; CCBATCH biogeochemical model for stability constant computation.
extractions.finding: Stability constants were similar across whole cells, cell walls, and EPS; sorption patterns explained by combining surface complexation with aqueous Np(V) speciation; log K values similar to aqueous carboxyl and N-containing ligands.
extractions.dataset: Shewanella alga strain BrY (whole cells, cell wall, EPS), Np(V)
extractions.metric: Stability constants (log K), deprotonation constants (pKa)
extractions.baseline: Aqueous carboxyl and N-containing carboxyl ligands
extractions.result: log K 1.8-3.9 for various surface functional groups

extractions.problem: Data-dependent acquisition incompletely samples peptide ions in complex protein mixtures like whole cell lysates in shotgun proteomics.
extractions.method: Statistical model to predict sampling levels for mixtures of specific complexity, relating spectral sampling to relative protein abundance.
extractions.finding: 10 analyses required for 95% saturation of protein identifications in yeast lysate; spectral sampling shows a linear relationship with relative protein abundance over 2 orders of magnitude.
extractions.dataset: Yeast soluble cell lysate
extractions.metric: Sampling saturation level, spectral count, linear dynamic range
extractions.result: 95% saturation at 10 analyses; 2 orders of magnitude linear dynamic range

extractions.problem: Determining whether the switch from whole-cell to acellular pertussis vaccines will affect protection against B. parapertussis infections.
extractions.method: Murine respiratory model comparing Dutch whole-cell vaccine and B. pertussis fimbriae (serotype-2 and -3) against B. pertussis and B. parapertussis infections.
extractions.finding: The whole-cell vaccine and B. pertussis fimbriae protected against both B. pertussis and B. parapertussis, though less efficiently against B. parapertussis; fimbriae were as efficacious as WCV against B. pertussis, indicating antigenic distinction between species' fimbriae.
extractions.dataset: Murine respiratory model, B. pertussis, B. parapertussis
extractions.metric: Protection efficacy (colonization reduction)
extractions.result: Fimbriae as efficacious as WCV against B. pertussis; lower efficacy against B. parapertussis
